reasoning:
Viswanathan Anand is largely retired from classical chess, meaning his FIDE rating is mostly frozen. For his ranking to be 'as high or higher' (i.e., a numerically smaller or equal rank), he relies either on his rank staying exactly the same or on players above him losing rating points and dropping below him. Because there are many more active players below him striving to improve their ratings than there are above him, an inactive player's ranking faces a natural downward drift over time. 

For the short-term November 2 date, there is a tangible chance Wikipedia has not yet been updated with the November 1st list, in which case his ranking would be exactly identical to the October 26 baseline, resolving to YES. Even if updated, the chance of his rank changing significantly in one month is moderate, leaving a high probability of him holding his position or benefiting from another player's rating drop. Consequently, the first date aligns closely with the base rate. For the November 25 date, the November list will definitely be up, reducing the 'Wikipedia delay' effect, thus slightly lowering the probability. By January 2026 (a 3-month horizon), this structural downward drift makes it more likely than not that at least one younger or more active player will have passed his frozen rating, dropping the probability below 50%.
